rounds_summary.attempted_route: Load model CSVs with heuristic column detection; load rule .py files via importlib and call predict/apply/rule by name; match raw task strings directly to model CSV keys.
rounds_summary.observation: All 139 participants → Unknown/Unknown. Raw task strings ('Some;A;B/All;B;C') never matched encoded CSV keys ('AA1'); rule modules silently raised and returned 0.

rounds_summary.attempted_route: Parse raw task strings into AIOE+figure syl codes; detect model CSV format (row-indexed vs wide-matrix); pre-compute rule predictions by trying predict/apply/rule/apply_rule function names.
rounds_summary.observation: Model matching fixed (64 syl codes each). Rule modules all failed: 'attempted relative import with no known parent package'; all 139 participants assigned default rule 'negativity' → 2 rows output. Eval: False.

rounds_summary.attempted_route: Added ccobra class instantiation (find subclass with .predict()), CCobraItem calls, and module-level dict lookup (PREDICTIONS/TABLE) as rule-prediction fallbacks; added stderr diagnostics.
rounds_summary.observation: Stderr confirms all 6 rule modules: 0 non-empty predictions. Relative import error persisted. Rule column stuck at 'negativity' by tie-break → 3 rows output. Eval: False.

ruled_out_routes: importlib.util.spec_from_file_location on rule .py files: relative imports fail without a known parent package regardless of sys.path manipulation.
Heuristic function-name probing (predict/apply/rule/apply_rule) on dynamically loaded rule modules: all six modules expose no callable at module level.
ccobra.CCobraModel subclass instantiation + CCobraItem.predict(): find_model_class returns None for all six rule modules when loaded outside their package.
Module-level dict lookup (PREDICTIONS/TABLE/pred) on rule modules: none of the six modules expose a top-level lookup table accessible after failed import.
synthesised_diagnosis: Across all three rounds the rule modules (negativity, figural, emptystart, particularity, atmosphere, partneg) use intra-package relative imports. Loading them with importlib.util outside their package context always raises 'attempted relative import with no known parent package', producing empty prediction sets. No amount of function-name guessing, class introspection, or sys.path patching can fix a relative import that requires a live parent package. The model side worked from round 2 onward; only the rule side is broken.
rationale: The relative-import error is a Python package-loading constraint, not a logic error. All three rounds patched symptoms (adding sys.path entries, fallback strategies) rather than the root cause: the rule modules must be imported within their declared package hierarchy.
recommended_alternative: Use importlib.import_module with the full dotted package path after inserting the scripts/indiv_table parent into sys.path: set sys.path to include the 'scripts' directory, then call importlib.import_module('indiv_table.rules.negativity') etc. Alternatively, use subprocess.run(['python', '-c', 'import sys; sys.path.insert(0,"scripts"); from indiv_table.rules import negativity; ...'], capture_output=True) to run rule queries inside the correct package context.
